status: Fixed in encoding touched multi-file parser lane
finding: `npm run check:encoding:touched -- --files <many files>` can pass while reporting only the first file in `evidence.files`, which makes the guard hard to trust for multi-file edits.
expectedBehavior: The guard should either verify and report every explicit `--files` entry, or clearly distinguish "checked files" from "display sample" so agents know the full touched set was covered.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by teaching `scripts/check-encoding-touched.ts` to consume every non-option token after `--files`, while preserving CSV and `--files=<csv>` input. Verification: `npm run check:encoding:touched -- --files scripts/check-encoding-touched.ts scripts/validate-script-parity.ts` now reports both files in `evidence.files`; `node --strip-types scripts/validate-script-parity.ts --mode validate` includes a multi-file probe.
followUp: Keep the multi-token `--files` regression in script parity so encoding guard evidence cannot silently shrink back to a one-file sample.
